sourceDocuments.title: TSFPEWG G 3-600-01.01-18 with Change 1
sourceDocuments.role: Mission continuity path, smoke exhaust triggers, controls, and pressure objective.

sourceDocuments.title: Handbook of Smoke Control Engineering, Chapter 16
sourceDocuments.role: Steady axisymmetric plume, exhaust volume, plugholing, inlet separation, and makeup velocity equations.

sourceDocuments.title: Handbook of Smoke Control Engineering, Chapter 3
sourceDocuments.role: Flow, leakage area, flow coefficient, and pressure-difference relationships.

sourceDocuments.title: NFPA 92 (2024)
sourceDocuments.role: Rectangular inlet geometry and smoke control criteria cross-checks used by the source spreadsheet.

requirements.requirement: Classify the information technology equipment mission and mission assurance category before selecting the protection path.
requirements.appResponse: Decision Path step 1 selects Chapter 2, 3, 4, 5, or 6 based on mission, MAC, and facility type.

requirements.requirement: Provide smoke exhaust from the information technology equipment room to the exterior unless a direct outside door supports a pre-positioned portable fan approach.
requirements.trigger: Chapter 2 baseline and paths that inherit Chapter 2
requirements.appResponse: Decision Path identifies permanent exhaust or the MAC III portable fan exception path.

requirements.requirement: Size exhaust for a smoke layer interface or concentration objective and keep makeup less than exhaust to limit spread to adjacent spaces.
requirements.appResponse: Trace steps F-001 through F-007 calculate exhaust; Compliance Checklist checks makeup less than exhaust.

requirements.requirement: Evaluate the referenced 12 Pa / 0.05 in. w.g. pressure-differential objective with respect to adjacent spaces.
requirements.appResponse: Pressure Helper and trace steps F-011/F-012 estimate pressure and solve required imbalance/helper values.

requirements.requirement: Use dedicated smoke exhaust separate from normal IT room air handling; makeup may be dedicated or normal HVAC and brought in below the design smoke layer interface.
requirements.appResponse: Reviewer Basis requires HVAC/fire-mode and activation-sequence evidence before a final calculation package.

requirements.requirement: Control smoke exhaust from the fire alarm system, include required smoke/fire damper isolation, manual deactivation, and fire-protection power supply features.
requirements.trigger: Permanent smoke exhaust path without clean agent manual-only override
requirements.appResponse: Decision Path and Reviewer Basis require the activation sequence and control basis to be documented.

requirements.requirement: MAC II facilities follow Chapter 2 as modified by Chapter 3; Alarm detection level activates smoke exhaust except where otherwise indicated.
requirements.trigger: MAC II, typical occupied or unoccupied facilities
requirements.appResponse: Decision Path routes MAC II to Chapter 3 and records Alarm-level activation basis.

requirements.requirement: Mission-critical/MAC I spaces require clean agent supplemental fire suppression; clean-agent activation does not activate smoke exhaust; smoke exhaust is manual-only from outside the IT room.
requirements.trigger: Mission-critical / MAC I or clean-agent scenario
requirements.appResponse: Decision Path routes MAC I to Chapter 4 and forces the report to document manual-only smoke exhaust activation.

requirements.requirement: Remote continuously occupied facilities follow the appropriate Chapter 2/3/4 path; remote unoccupied facilities follow Chapter 4 as modified, and Alarm detection activates smoke exhaust unless clean agent is provided.
requirements.appResponse: Decision Path routes Remote selections to Chapter 5 and flags clean-agent/activation evidence in Reviewer Basis.

requirements.requirement: Modular data centers follow Chapter 2 as modified by Chapter 6; mission-critical modular facilities use Chapter 3 detection and clean agent in accordance with Chapter 4.
requirements.appResponse: Decision Path routes Modular selections to Chapter 6 and leaves clean-agent/detection proof in Reviewer Basis.

requirements.requirement: Show all variables, formulas, substitutions, and limitations for the selected smoke exhaust calculation path.
requirements.appResponse: Variable Register, Formula Register, Calculation Trace, Excel Mode formula path, and Raw JSON are included in the report.

requirements.requirement: Document equation applicability, design fire basis, AHJ/insurer assumptions, and independent validation before submittal.
requirements.trigger: All scenarios intended for project use
requirements.appResponse: Data Quality remains Review or Blocked until reviewer-basis entries and confirmations are completed.
